source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The diagram on the right shows a square with side $3 \mathrm{~cm}$ inside a square with side $7 \mathrm{~cm}$ and another square with side $5 \mathrm{~cm}$ which intersects the first two squares. What is the difference between the area of the black region and the total area of the grey regions? <image1>
extraction_notes: raw_answer_text mapped from answer letter D using options
solution contains an additional image URL

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'The diagram on the right shows' and asks about black and grey regions, which are not fully recoverable from text alone
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the sample is intended to depend on the image
the task involves intersections among three squares and region colors, both of which are visual/spatial properties
multi_step: solving requires combining information from three squares with side lengths 3 cm, 5 cm, and 7 cm
the question asks for the difference between the area of one set of regions and the total area of another set, implying at least area decomposition plus comparison/subtraction
the intersecting-square setup suggests joint geometric and visual reasoning rather than a single direct lookup
verifiability: answer_text is a specific numeric result with units: '$15 \\mathrm{~cm}^{2}$'
choices are provided and the stated answer matches one of the options (D), making final-answer checking straightforward
asset_registry_record shows question_present=true, answer_present=true, and registry_passed=true

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, and asset registry passed with no recorded issues
the sample has very high image dependency, and the required diagram asset exists, so the core semantics are available
verifiability is high because the answer is a specific numeric result with units and can be checked directly
although the diagram geometry is not fully specified in text and no solution explanation is provided, these are not blocking because the primary image is available
